channel.title: min-max on Noam Zaks
channel.description: Recent content in min-max on Noam Zaks

item.title: Triangle Areas
item.description: Given a group $ S $ of points $ (x,y) \in \mathbb R ^ 2 $ such that every three points in $ S $ form a triangle (aren’t collinear) of area at most $ 1 $, show that there is some triangle with area at most 2 which bounds $ S $. Solution The idea is to look at the three points which make the triangle with the largest area, and note the following observation:
